title: High pressure seal made of metallic like material
abstract: Sealing ring for sealing fluid, gas, or steam at high pressure in one or two flow directions between two surfaces with leaning sealing grooves and a sealing ring without split and with a very small cross section that can accept leaning grooves and is possibly to month in groves in one piece and with pressure balancing in two directions and only because of the form of the cross section can control the sealing rings contact pressure to be less than 50% of sealed pressure and with low wear, friction and leakage even when the size is small like in sealing gaps.
claims:
Claims 1. A high pressure sealing ring, without split or with a closed split in the circumference and made of metal or metal like material and sealing between a first and second annular surface and each of said sealing rings is working in an annular sealing groove in one of said surfaces and sealing against the other surface, with one of two types of sealing rings. Sealing with its outer diameter, named piston sealing ring or sealing with its inner diameter, named rod sealing ring Said sealing ring with its cross section is principle working the same for both piston and rod sealing rings The cross section of said annular ring and its shape or form must be the only thing that is controlling how sealed medium of fluid, gas or steam is working when the sealing ring is sealing between said two annular surfaces and consequently not the shape or form of the sealing groove. The maximum widths of the sealing rings sealing groove must always be wider than the maximum widths of the sealing rings cross section. The surface of the cross section of the sealing ring that is pushed against said annular surface it is sealing against, has a groove named the balancing groove between two lengths named the sealing lengths. The two sealing lengths are each divided in two lengths. One length is the widths of the two contacts surface on the sealing rings cross section, and the other lengths is going to a narrow contact point or line surface where the widths of the sealing rings cross section is maximum. On both side of the contact point or line surface are the side surface so shaped or formed that the sealing rings cross section not can be in contact with the side wall of the sealing groove, even when the there is a angle under 90Â° in two directions between the surface the sealing ring is sealing against and the side wall of the sealing groove. 

 The distains between the contacting surfaces on the sealing rings cross section and one narrow contact point or contact line of the side surfaces of the cross section, must preferably be the same as said distains named sealing lengths. The annular side of the sealing rings cross section that not is in contact with the surface the seal is sealing against is close but newer in contact with the bottom of the sealing groove. In said balancing groove in the sealing rings cross section is a flow and pressure canal going to another groove on the other side of the cross section of the sealing ring. On both side of said other groove are two lengths on each side with cylindrical form and with accurate the same diameter. The balancing groove and said other groove are made about as deep as one third of the cross section maximum thickness (H). 2. A sealing ring and sealing groove as in Claims 1. wherein the cross section of the sealing ring has sizes: width (W) around and below 2 mm thickness (H) around and below 0,5 mm. 3. A sealing ring and sealing groove as in Claim 1. and 2. wherein the sealing ring for both piston seals and rod seals are double acting. To change the cross section of the sealing ring to be a single acting seal with ability to seal only in one pressure drop direction must now the Claims 1. be changed, so that the canal between the balancing groove and said other groove not is claimed and replaced with a claim with a axial groove in one of two sealing lengths going from the balancing groove to one side wall of the cross section. The surface with the axial groove is now named the supporting lengths as it still is a contacting surface but not a sealing surface. 4. A sealing ring and a sealing groove as in previous claims for use in spool valves and other valves with size Width (W) around and below 1.2 mm. Thickness (H) around and below 0,35 mm. 5. A sealing ring and a sealing groove as in previous claims with a sealing ring made of ballbearing steel and harden to about 700 Vickers. 6. A sealing ring and sealing groove as in Claim 1. wherein all contacting surface has a surface roughness that is fine and about Ra=0,l Î¼ÏÎ¹ or finer. 7. The sealing ring and sealing groove as in all preceding claims wherein if the sealing ring is made of harden steel must the surface it is sealing against also be made of steel. 8. The sealing ring and sealing groove as in all preceding claims wherein if the sealing ring is made of hard aluminum must the surface it is sealing against also be made of aluminum. 9. The sealing ring and the sealing groove as in all preceding claims wherein contact surfaces of the sealing ring is covered by a thin surface layer of a wear resistant material, like Balinite C, on steel or the like.
